findings.title: 12-hour clock format drops AM/PM indicator
findings.description: The time format string is 'E Â· d MMMM Â· hh:mm'. The 'hh' token produces 12-hour clock hours (01â€“12) but there is no 'a' (AM/PM) modifier. At 2:30 PM the display will show '02:30', indistinguishable from 2:30 AM. For a medical app where timestamps on bowel-movement and food logs are clinically significant, this is a meaningful correctness issue â€” not a cosmetic one.
findings.fix: Change the format string to include the AM/PM indicator: `format(now, "E Â· d MMMM Â· hh:mm a")`, or switch to 24-hour time with `HH:mm`.

findings.title: Full reproductive health type hierarchy retained despite the feature being stripped
findings.description: This PR's stated purpose is to strip reproductive health. Yet `ReproductiveHealthSettings`, `PregnancyStatus`, `MenopauseStatus`, `ReproductiveCyclePhase` and all their related string-literal union types (lines 54â€“61) are still exported from domain.ts. The `HealthProfile` interface still embeds `reproductiveHealth: ReproductiveHealthSettings` (line 239). The `DEFAULT_HEALTH_PROFILE` in store.ts still initialises a full `reproductiveHealth` object (lines 105â€“129 of store.ts). These are not passively harmless: they are serialised into every user's Convex cloud profile document, meaning the schema continues to carry and transmit reproductive health data even after the UI is removed.
findings.fix: Remove the `reproductiveHealth` field from `HealthProfile`, remove the `ReproductiveHealthSettings` interface and all related type aliases from domain.ts, and remove the `reproductiveHealth` initialiser from `DEFAULT_HEALTH_PROFILE` in store.ts. If a migration is needed to clean existing Convex documents, plan it separately.

findings.title: DEFAULT_HEALTH_PROFILE initialises a complete reproductive health sub-object
findings.description: Even though no Settings UI exposes reproductive health any more, `DEFAULT_HEALTH_PROFILE` initialises a 20-field `reproductiveHealth` object (lines 105â€“129). Any call that spreads or merges this default will silently write these fields to Convex. This is a consequence of F002 not yet being cleaned up, but it has independent impact: it inflates every new user's stored profile.
findings.fix: Remove the `reproductiveHealth` key from `DEFAULT_HEALTH_PROFILE` in tandem with the domain.ts cleanup described in F002.

findings.title: Dead CSS for .glass-card-repro and .settings-panel-repro retained
findings.description: The CSS file defines token variables (`--section-repro`, `--section-repro-muted`, `--section-repro-border`, `--section-repro-glow`) in both light and dark theme blocks, the `.glass-card-repro` class (line 691), and the `.settings-panel-repro` class (lines 832â€“837). After the feature strip these classes are only referenced in two settings-component files (`SettingsTile.tsx`, `SettingsSelect.tsx`) which still reference them â€” confirming the settings components also need a cleanup pass. The CSS is dead weight that also signals an incomplete removal.
findings.fix: Remove the `--section-repro-*` token variables from both theme blocks, `.glass-card-repro`, `.settings-panel-repro`, and their light-theme variants from index.css. Coordinate with the cleanup of `SettingsTile.tsx` and `SettingsSelect.tsx`.

findings.title: Dead @keyframes for transit map retained in CSS
findings.description: Two keyframe animations that were exclusively for the deleted transit map feature â€” `station-pulse` (line 1123) and `you-are-here-pulse` (line 1137) â€” remain in index.css with their source comments explicitly stating they belong to the transit map. Since the transit map page is deleted, these keyframes are dead code.
findings.fix: Delete the `@keyframes station-pulse` and `@keyframes you-are-here-pulse` blocks from index.css.

findings.title: UiMigrationLab route still registered in production router
findings.description: The route `/ui-migration-lab` and its lazy import (`UiMigrationLabPage`) remain in routeTree.tsx (lines 36, 435â€“445). This route is listed in the router's addChildren call (line 454), which means it is accessible to any authenticated user who knows the URL. If the page is a development/debugging artifact (its name strongly suggests so), it should not be reachable in production.
findings.fix: Either gate the route behind a `FEATURE_FLAGS` flag and only register it in development builds, or delete the route, import, and page file entirely. If it must remain for migration purposes, add a comment explaining its temporary nature and an owner/deadline.

findings.title: FEATURE_FLAGS is an empty object â€” the module serves no purpose in its current state
findings.description: `FEATURE_FLAGS` is exported as `{} as const`. With no flags defined, every import of this module is dead code. F006 above demonstrates a case where a feature flag should exist but doesn't. The module is fine as an architecture pattern, but having it empty while unexpanded routes exist is a tension.
findings.fix: Either add the `uiMigrationLab` flag here (e.g. `uiMigrationLab: import.meta.env.DEV`) and use it to gate F006, or add a comment explaining that new flags go here so reviewers understand it is intentionally empty.

findings.title: Custom Tailwind class max-w-440 is not defined anywhere
findings.description: `className="mx-auto max-w-440 pb-8"` uses `max-w-440` which does not correspond to any Tailwind v4 built-in utility, is not defined in index.css, and is not in any @theme block. Tailwind v4 will silently generate nothing for this class if it cannot infer it. The Track page will have no enforced maximum width, causing it to stretch edge-to-edge on wide screens. The class appears in `dist/assets/index-CEAEtV4Y.css`, suggesting it was previously defined elsewhere but has been lost.
findings.fix: Define the utility in index.css (e.g., `@layer utilities { .max-w-440 { max-width: 110rem; } }`) or replace it with the nearest standard Tailwind value such as `max-w-screen-2xl` (96rem) or `max-w-[110rem]` as an arbitrary value.

findings.title: Commented-out Dr Poo auto-analysis code creates misleading dead code fragments
findings.description: Lines 95â€“99 show `sendNow` is extracted from `useAiInsights()` and labelled 'TEMPORARILY DISABLED: Dr Poo auto-analysis on BM log'. The hook call and destructure are live (triggering subscription), but the actual trigger code (`triggerAnalysis`) is commented out at lines 321â€“325. This means the hook is subscribed but only `sendNow` (the manual trigger) is used. If the intent is that `useAiInsights` runs only for manual triggers, the inline comment is misleading and leaves readers uncertain whether there is an active subscription side-effect. Additionally, `useAiPreferences` is imported-then-commented out (line 35â€“37) which pollutes the import block.
findings.fix: If the auto-analysis is genuinely disabled, remove the commented-out import block for `useAiPreferences` (line 35â€“37) and the commented `triggerAnalysis` block (lines 321â€“325). Leave a tracked work-queue item for re-enabling it, not inline TODO comments.

findings.title: Navigation link icon and label are both aria-hidden â€” link has no accessible name on mobile
findings.description: In `GlobalHeader`, each nav link `<Link>` has `aria-label={item.label}` on the link element (line 220). However, the icon element has `aria-hidden="true"` (line 227) and the text `<span>` also has `aria-hidden="true"` (line 228). On desktop (`md:inline`) the label is visible and the `aria-label` on the `<Link>` provides the accessible name. On mobile the span is `hidden` and the icon is `aria-hidden`, but the `<Link>` still has its `aria-label`, so it should work. The real issue is that the `NavigationMenuLink` wraps the `<Link>` â€” the link itself has the label but is nested inside another interactive element (`NavigationMenuLink asChild`). Depending on how shadcn's `asChild` composition renders, the screen reader might encounter nested interactive elements, which is invalid HTML and confusing to assistive technology.
findings.fix: Verify the rendered DOM for the nav links. If `NavigationMenuLink asChild` passes through to the `Link` cleanly (as intended), confirm with a quick accessibility audit. If nesting is the outcome, restructure so the `<Link>` is the outermost interactive element.

findings.title: Shared JSX elements computed every render without memoization
findings.description: Lines 483â€“522 compute `todayStatusRow`, `quickCapture`, `aiInsightsSection`, and `todayLog` as plain JSX variables on every render of `TrackPage`. These are not `useMemo`-wrapped. TrackPage is a large component with many state variables; any one of them (e.g. `now` updating every minute via the timer) will re-compute these entire subtrees on each tick, even though their props haven't changed.
findings.fix: Wrap each of these shared element variables in `useMemo` with their relevant prop dependencies, or extract them into separate child components with stable prop references. Given the number of hooks in TrackPage, consider whether a further breakdown into smaller components would reduce the re-render surface area.

findings.title: TODO comment for meal schedule config left in production types
findings.description: Line 187 (in the `DEFAULT_AI_PREFERENCES` definition block) contains: `// TODO: expose these as user-configurable defaults in the onboarding flow.` TODOs embedded in type/constant files are invisible to project management tooling and can persist indefinitely. This one has been here since the domain types were authored.
findings.fix: Remove the inline TODO and create a tracked work-queue item in docs/WORK-QUEUE.md instead.

findings.title: sanitizeLogData casts sanitized data with 'as FoodLogData' after deep sanitization â€” structural integrity not verified
findings.description: `sanitizeUnknownStringsDeep(data)` returns `unknown` and each switch branch immediately casts the result (e.g. `const d = sanitized as FoodLogData`). The deep sanitizer trims strings but does not validate field presence or types. If `data` arrives malformed (e.g. a food log missing `items`, or a digestion log with a non-numeric `bristolCode`), the cast will succeed at the TypeScript level and the resulting `ConvexLogData` object will pass to the Convex mutation where the server validator will reject it â€” producing an opaque error rather than a clear client-side failure. The server will catch it, but the client error will be generic.
findings.fix: Add lightweight structural assertions before each cast (e.g. `if (!Array.isArray((data as FoodLogData).items)) throw new Error('food log missing items array')` or use a zod/valibot schema). Alternatively, accept that the server is the canonical validation layer and document this explicitly in the function's JSDoc.

findings.title: Storage key name 'PDH-openai-key' will break if the provider changes
findings.description: The IDB key is named `PDH-openai-key` but the app supports multiple AI models (Gemini etc. per `src/types/domain.ts` `AiModel` type). If the user switches to a non-OpenAI model and the key name stays as-is, a future migration or provider refactor must know to rename or migrate this key â€” the name becomes misleading and potentially a maintenance trap.
findings.fix: Rename to a provider-agnostic key such as `PDH-ai-api-key`. This is a one-time IDB migration (or a new key with fallback read of the old key).

findings.title: TodayLabel duplicates the self-correcting clock timer pattern already in Track.tsx
findings.description: The `TodayLabel` component (lines 386â€“407) implements its own self-correcting clock timer (`setTimeout` + recursive `tick`) that is identical in logic to the one in `TrackPage` (lines 114â€“131). This is exact code duplication of a non-trivial pattern. A bug or improvement to the timer logic would need to be applied in two places.
findings.fix: Extract the clock logic to a shared hook, e.g. `useCurrentTime(formatFn?)` in `src/hooks/useCurrentTime.ts`, and use it in both components.

findings.title: Stale comment references removed feature
findings.description: Line 381 reads: `// â”€â”€ FoodEntryPoints removed â€” replaced by FoodSafetyGrid (Bug #20) â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€`. This comment documents a historical change that has already been made. It no longer communicates anything actionable and adds noise to the file.
findings.fix: Remove the comment. Historical context like this belongs in git commit messages or ADRs, not inline code.

findings.title: prefers-reduced-motion media query does not cover stagger-reveal and aurora background animations
findings.description: The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at line 1649 disables several animations, but it misses `stagger-reveal` (which uses `fadeSlideUp` on every child element), `aurora-shift` (the continuously animating background gradient on `body::after`), and `card-glow-pulse`. Users with vestibular disorders who have reduced motion enabled will still experience the aurora background looping at 12s intervals and every page transition triggering stagger animations.
findings.fix: Add `stagger-reveal > *`, `body::after`, and `.animate-aurora` to the reduced-motion override block: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stagger-reveal > * { animation: none; } body::after { animation: none; } }`

findings.title: Test uses unsafe type assertion to inspect result shape
findings.description: Several test assertions cast result to `{ items: unknown[] }` or `{ items: Array<{ canonicalName?: string; unit: string | null }> }` using inline type assertions (e.g. lines 163, 205). While acceptable in tests, these widened casts mean the test will not catch a rename or shape change in the return type of `sanitizeLogData`. The test would still pass even if the returned shape changed.
findings.fix: Use typed expectations by importing `ConvexLogData` (or the food/fluid-specific subtypes) and asserting the result satisfies the type. Consider `expect(result).toMatchObject({ items: [...] })` patterns that are more robust.
